items.body:
🎯 **Agentic Issue Triage**

Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist.

**Summary:** You're reporting that `Azure.AI.OpenAI` 2.9.0-beta.1 removed the deployment/model parameter from `GetResponsesClient()`, breaking code that uses the `AsAIAgent()` extension from `Microsoft.Agents.AI.OpenAI` 1.0.0-rc4. The constructor parameter for specifying the model deployment no longer works and no alternative appears to be available.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Analysis</summary>

### What Changed
In `Azure.AI.OpenAI` 2.9.0-beta.1, `GetResponsesClient()` no longer accepts a deployment name as a constructor parameter. This aligns with changes in the upstream OpenAI Responses API where the model is specified per-request rather than at client construction time.

### Impact
The Agent Framework (`Microsoft.Agents.AI.OpenAI`) relies on `GetResponsesClient(deployment)` to wire up `AsAIAgent()`. Until the Agent Framework updates its integration to match the new pattern, this workflow is broken.

### Reproduction
```csharp
// This pattern no longer works in 2.9.0-beta.1
AIAgent agent = new AzureOpenAIClient(new Uri(_azureOpenAiEndpoint), new ApiKeyCredential(_azureOpenAiApiKey))
    .GetResponsesClient("o4-mini")
    .AsAIAgent(instructions: "You are a friendly assistant.", name: "MyAgentBackgroundResponse");
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔍 Debugging Suggestions</summary>

- Check the [Azure.AI.OpenAI changelog](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/openai/Azure.AI.OpenAI/CHANGELOG.md) for 2.9.0-beta.1 to see if there is a migration guide for the new Responses API pattern.
- Check if `ResponsesClient` now accepts model per-request (e.g., via `ResponseCreationOptions`) rather than at client construction time.
- As a workaround, consider pinning to `Azure.AI.OpenAI` 2.8.x until `Microsoft.Agents.AI.OpenAI` releases an update compatible with 2.9.0-beta.1.
- Watch the [Microsoft Agents SDK](https://github.com/microsoft/agents) repository for an update that handles the new `GetResponsesClient()` pattern.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category: `Client`** — High confidence. This is a client SDK behavior change, not a management or provisioning library issue.
- **Service: `OpenAI`** — High confidence. The issue explicitly involves `AzureOpenAIClient` and `GetResponsesClient()` from the `Azure.AI.OpenAI` package in `/sdk/openai/`.

</details>
